title: Judicial precedent
summary: 9084 — stare decisis, ratio decidendi, obiter dicta, and court hierarchy.
learningObjectives: Define stare decisis, ratio, and obiter.
Evaluate advantages and rigidity of precedent.

simpleExplanation.title: Judges follow past decisions
simpleExplanation.summary: Lower courts must follow binding ratio from higher courts — the doctrine of precedent ensures consistency but can slow reform.
steps: Supreme Court → Court of Appeal → Divisional → High → Crown/County.

sections.content: **Judicial precedent** (1.1.5) completes 1.1. Practise **ratio extraction** from case summaries.
